**Analysis of <missing 1>:**
The text surrounding <missing 1> is:
"Even if Bob divides $n'$ until $v_2 (n)=v_2 (a)$ , Alice will apply the same strategy and keep <missing 1> ."
The sentence describes Alice's strategy in the "General Case" where $v_2(a) = x$. The strategy involves adding $a$ to a number $n$ when $v_2(n) = v_2(a)$. The text previously states that this addition results in a new number $n'$ with $v_2(n') \geq v_2(a) + 1$. The goal of the strategy is to prevent the game from ending by ensuring there is always an even number on the board that Bob can divide. Specifically, the text says "Alice will apply the same strategy and keep [something]".
Looking at the available options:
- Expression 1: $v_2 (n)$
- Expression 2: $v_2 (n') \geq v_2 (a)+1$

The sentence structure "keep [expression]" suggests maintaining a condition. The condition established immediately before is that the valuation of the number becomes at least $v_2(a)+1$. If Alice keeps the valuation at this level (or higher), Bob cannot reduce it all the way to 0 (odd) without Alice intervening again to boost it. The phrase "keep $v_2(n') \geq v_2(a)+1$" fits the logical flow of maintaining the invariant that allows the game to continue.
